reviews.review_msg: As a guy he is perfect, really cares about you when you show up to office hours. I know we are in uni and that we should expect some hard tasks but his readings were very long to be quickly skimmed through in a 50 min lecture. His test is all about writing so get ready to write. A LOT! Tutorial attendance is mandatory.
Really fun to listen to, very old fashioned professor - like. Hard but enjoyable class.

Paul is a great lecturer. He is able to take boring topics and make them very interesting. He records his lectures in case you miss a class. He puts up vocabulary before every lecture that could appear on the exams. Although he is a tough grader his exams are fair. He cares about his students, and wants them to do well. Listen and you'll do well.

Dutton is a great prof. Very knowledgable, and seems to care about his students. However, you have to pay attention in lecture since there are no power points or anything to help you if you miss the class. Textbook is very very useful as well. Midterms and final were all written, so know your stuff. If you study you will be fine.

Absolutely loved Dr. O'Brien. Very clear with what she is looking for, supportive, caring, usually runs helpful workshops during office hours and is very flexible with setting up meetings outside of those times. I found the course fairly easy just because of how clear she is in her expectations, but be aware that there is still a lot to take in.

Dr. O'Brien is a sweet heart who puts in all her effort to engage and put interest in course material but often is vague with her expectations. Expect to write a ton, especially during exams and know the "historical significance" of everything. Textbook was only useful for readings in tutorials. Overall good class but high expectations are set.
